activity.description: Take a sunrise hot air balloon ride over the ancient capitals of the Cultural Triangle for a breathtaking aerial view of Sigiriya and the surrounding jungle. Includes pre-flight briefing, 1-hour flight, and post-flight breakfast.
Explore the backroads and tea trails of the Hill Country on a guided mountain biking tour. Includes bike fitting, multiple stops, and rest breaks.
Explore the rural villages of the Cultural Triangle on a guided mountain biking tour. Includes bike fitting, multiple stops, and rest breaks.

Embark on a challenging overnight hike to the sacred 7,243 ft peak to witness a breathtaking sunrise from a site holy to multiple religions. Includes 5-6 hour climb, sunrise viewing (30-45 mins), 3-4 hour descent, and rest stops.

Trek through the misty mountains and cloud forests of the Knuckles Mountain Range, a UNESCO heritage site, discovering unique flora and waterfalls. Includes guide, packed lunch, and multiple scenic viewpoints.
Enjoy a relatively easy and short hike near Ella with stunning panoramic views of the Ella Gap and surrounding tea-covered hills. Perfect for sunset viewing.
Embark on a rewarding half-day hike through tea plantations and jungle to reach the summit of Ella Rock for spectacular views. Allow time for photos and enjoying the panorama.
Climb Pidurangala, a massive rock located close to Sigiriya, for a less crowded but equally stunning view of the famous Lion Rock, especially at sunrise. Includes visit to cave temple at base.
Challenge yourself with rock climbing on the granite outcrops near Sigiriya, suitable for both beginners and experienced climbers. Includes equipment, certified instructor, and multiple routes.

Soar through the treetops on a zip-line course near Dambulla, offering an adrenaline rush and a unique perspective of the jungle and village landscape. Includes safety briefing and multiple zip lines.

activity.activity_name: Off-Road 4WD Safari in the Knuckles Range
activity.description: Take a 4WD vehicle on an off-road adventure through the rugged terrain of the Knuckles Range, passing remote villages and waterfalls. Includes driver/guide, photo stops, and picnic lunch.

Experience the final and arguably most scenic leg of the hill country railway from Badulla to Ella, winding through lush greenery and mountains. The famous Nine Arches Bridge view is along this route.
Trek to the top of Sri Lanka's second-highest waterfall for breathtaking views and the reward of natural infinity pools. Includes guide, swimming time, and packed lunch.
Learn to surf or hone your skills on the long, gentle waves of Weligama Bay, a hub for surf schools and backpackers. Includes 1.5-2 hour lesson plus practice time.
Test your skills on the point breaks of Arugam Bay, one of the world's top surf destinations, with a laid-back vibe and consistent swells. Best at low to mid tide.
Join local families in Colombo's favorite ocean-side promenade to fly kites, snack on street food, and watch the sunset over the Indian Ocean.
Take a leisurely walk along the ancient walls of Galle Fort at sunset, enjoying views of the ocean and the historic lighthouse. Includes photo stops and watching the sunset.
Experience the perfect wave at Hiriketiya, a picturesque bay near Tangalle that has become a favorite for surfers of all levels in a stunning setting. Best in the morning before wind picks up.
Explore the coral reefs and underwater wrecks off the coast of Trincomalee, a prime spot for scuba diving on the east coast. Includes two dives with surface interval and equipment.
Relax and swim on the long, flat expanse of Nilaveli Beach, known for its shallow, calm turquoise waters and soft white sand.

Visit the Kosgoda Turtle Hatchery or simply walk the beach at night (with a guide) to witness sea turtles nesting or hatchlings making their way to the ocean. Timing depends on tides and nesting season.

activity.activity_name: Beach Hopping in the South Coast
activity.description: Spend a day visiting multiple distinct beaches along the south coast, from the busy shores of Hikkaduwa to the quiet coves of Talalla. Includes travel and lunch stop.
Snorkel directly from the beach in Hikkaduwa to see a variety of reef fish and, with luck, sea turtles in the Hikkaduwa Coral Sanctuary. Best at high tide for better access.
Enjoy the rolling waves of Bentota Beach on a bodyboard, a fun and easy activity for all ages in this resort town.
Paddle through the calm waters of the Negombo Lagoon, spotting diverse birdlife and getting a glimpse of traditional fishing communities. Best in the early morning for bird activity.
Experience international-standard table games and slot machines at Bally's Colombo, one of the city's premier casinos located in a luxury hotel. Open 24 hours, but atmosphere peaks at night.
Try your luck at the Bellagio Colombo, a well-known casino complex offering a range of gaming options including roulette, blackjack, and baccarat. Open 24 hours.
Enjoy a sophisticated gaming experience at Marina Casino in Colombo, which features a variety of tables and a private club atmosphere. Open 24 hours.
Experience the gaming floor at Stardust Casino, another prominent casino in Colombo offering a range of betting options and entertainment. Open 24 hours.

Ascend the ancient rock fortress of Sigiriya, exploring its frescoes, mirror wall, and the ruins of a 5th-century palace perched on top. Includes queue time, slow climb, and time to appreciate the archaeological significance.

activity.activity_name: Visiting the Temple of the Sacred Tooth Relic
activity.description: Experience the reverence at this sacred Buddhist temple in Kandy, which houses the relic of the tooth of the Buddha. Best during morning or evening pooja (offering) ceremonies.

Wander through a series of five impressive cave temples carved into a massive rock at Dambulla, filled with Buddha statues and colorful murals. Includes walk up to the caves and exploration of all five temples.

activity.activity_name: Discovering the Ancient City of Polonnaruwa
activity.description: Explore the well-preserved ruins of Polonnaruwa, the medieval capital of Sri Lanka, with its royal palaces and Buddhist shrines. Best done by bicycle to cover the large site efficiently.

activity.activity_name: Exploring the Ancient City of Anuradhapura
activity.description: Wander through the sprawling sacred city of Anuradhapura, home to massive dagobas (stupas) and the sacred Sri Maha Bodhi tree. Requires transport between major sites.

Get lost in the charming streets of Galle Fort, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, exploring its Dutch-colonial architecture, boutiques, and cafes. Includes multiple stops at shops and photo opportunities.
Admire the striking red-and-white striped facade of this historic mosque in the heart of Colombo's Pettah market district. Non-Muslims cannot enter, but exterior photography is a must.

activity.activity_name: Exploring the National Museum of Colombo
activity.description: Discover Sri Lanka's rich history and art, including the crown jewels and the throne of the last king, at the country's largest museum in Colombo.

activity.activity_name: Attending the Esala Perahera in Kandy
activity.description: Witness one of Asia's most spectacular cultural pageants, a 10-day festival of dancers, drummers, and decorated elephants in Kandy (July/August). Arrive early for good seats.
Visit this massive colonial fort in the north, built by the Portuguese and later modified by the Dutch, offering a glimpse into Sri Lanka's strategic importance.
Immerse yourself in the vibrant Tamil Hindu culture at this significant and architecturally stunning temple in Jaffna. Best during festival times or daily pooja ceremonies.

activity.activity_name: Strolling Through the Jaffna Public Library
activity.description: Pay your respects at this iconic symbol of Tamil culture and learning at the Jaffna Public Library, which was rebuilt after its destruction and stands as a beacon of resilience.

Perched on a cliff in Trincomalee, this ancient Hindu temple offers breathtaking ocean views and is a significant pilgrimage site. Best visited in the morning for cooler temperatures and better light.

activity.activity_name: Visiting the Dutch Reformed Church in Galle
activity.description: Step inside this simple yet historic church within Galle Fort, a reminder of the Dutch colonial era with its original wooden pulpit and floor tombs.

activity.activity_name: Exploring the National Railway Museum in Colombo
activity.description: Discover the history of Sri Lanka's famous railway, including vintage locomotives and carriages at the National Railway Museum in Colombo.

activity.activity_name: Visiting the Gangaramaya Temple in Colombo
activity.description: Explore this eclectic and fascinating temple in Colombo, which combines modern architecture with traditional design and houses a unique collection of artifacts.

activity.activity_name: Experiencing a Pooja at a Hindu Temple (Colombo)
activity.description: Witness a Hindu ceremony (pooja) at a colorful temple like the Old Kathiresan Temple in Colombo. Morning poojas are most authentic.

activity.activity_name: Experiencing a Pooja at a Hindu Temple (Matale)
activity.description: Witness a Hindu ceremony (pooja) at the Muthumariamman Temple in Matale. Morning poojas are most authentic.

activity.activity_name: Visiting the Seetha Amman Temple in Nuwara Eliya
activity.description: Explore this unique Hindu temple dedicated to Sita, which is believed to be the site of her captivity in the epic Ramayana.

activity.activity_name: Discovering the Gal Vihara in Polonnaruwa
activity.description: Marvel at the ancient rock-cut Buddha statues at Gal Vihara, a group of four magnificent images carved from a single granite face.
Wander around this historic fort in Trincomalee, which still serves as a military base, and visit the ancient Koneswaram temple within its grounds.
Embark on a guided tour in Colombo to sample local delicacies like kottu roti, hoppers, samosas, and short eats from street vendors. Evening is when street food culture comes alive.
Embark on a guided tour in Kandy to sample local delicacies like kottu roti, hoppers, samosas, and short eats from street vendors. Evening is when street food culture comes alive.

Learn the secrets of Sri Lankan cuisine in a hands-on cooking class, often including a visit to a local market to select fresh ingredients. Includes market tour, cooking instruction, and eating your creations.

Learn the secrets of Sri Lankan cuisine in a hands-on cooking class, often including a visit to a local market to select fresh ingredients. Includes market tour, cooking instruction, and eating your creations.
Enjoy a classic colonial experience with a traditional afternoon high tea at the historic Grand Hotel in Nuwara Eliya. Includes selection of teas, sandwiches, pastries, and scones.
Dine on freshly caught lobster, crab, and prawns at a beachfront restaurant in Mirissa. Best at sunset for atmosphere.
Dine on freshly caught lobster, crab, and prawns at a beachfront restaurant in Negombo. Best at sunset for atmosphere.
Dine on freshly caught lobster, crab, and prawns at a beachfront restaurant in Unawatuna. Best at sunset for atmosphere.

activity.activity_name: Tea Tasting at a Plantation (Nuwara Eliya)
activity.description: Visit a tea factory in the Hill Country, learn about the tea-making process, and participate in a guided tea-tasting session.
Visit a tea factory in the Hill Country, learn about the tea-making process, and participate in a guided tea-tasting session.
Sample locally brewed craft beers at microbreweries in Colombo. Includes flight of beers and tour of facilities.
Sample locally brewed craft beers at the Lion Brewery in Negombo. Includes flight of beers and tour of facilities.
Dine at a local Sri Lankan "hotel" - which are actually hole-in-the-wall eateries - for an authentic and affordable meal of rice and curry or kottu roti. Lunch is prime time for rice and curry.
Dine at a local Sri Lankan "hotel" - which are actually hole-in-the-wall eateries - for an authentic and affordable meal of rice and curry or kottu roti. Lunch is prime time for rice and curry.

activity.activity_name: Visiting a Spice Garden in Matale
activity.description: Tour a fragrant spice garden near Matale to see how cinnamon, cardamom, pepper, and other spices are grown and used in Ayurvedic medicine. Includes guided tour and optional Ayurvedic treatments.
Stay hydrated by drinking fresh king coconut water from a roadside seller, a refreshing and healthy experience unique to the tropics, found everywhere, especially along the south coast.
Sample the spicy and flavorful Jaffna-style crab curry, a signature dish of the northern region, best enjoyed at a dedicated crab restaurant. Lunch is the main meal in Jaffna.

activity.activity_name: Visiting a Local Fruit Market (Colombo Pettah)
activity.description: Immerse yourself in the colors and aromas of a local fruit market, trying exotic fruits like rambutan, mangosteen, and wood apple in Pettah Market. Morning is when markets are freshest and least crowded.
Experience a classic evening meal of freshly grilled fish, prawns, or lobster right on the sand at a laid-back beach shack in Mirissa.
Experience a classic evening meal of freshly grilled fish, prawns, or lobster right on the sand at a laid-back beach shack in Unawatuna.
Take a short workshop to learn how to make appa (hoppers) – the bowl-shaped rice flour crepes that are a breakfast staple – from a local chef.
Take a short workshop to learn how to make appa (hoppers) – the bowl-shaped rice flour crepes that are a breakfast staple – from a local chef.

activity.activity_name: Jeep Safari in Yala National Park (Morning)
activity.description: Embark on a thrilling jeep safari in Yala National Park, famous for having one of the highest densities of leopards in the world. Early morning offers best wildlife viewing when animals are most active.

activity.activity_name: Jeep Safari in Yala National Park (Evening)
activity.description: Embark on a thrilling jeep safari in Yala National Park, famous for having one of the highest densities of leopards in the world. Evening safari as animals become active again.

activity.activity_name: Jeep Safari in Udawalawe National Park
activity.description: Go on a safari in Udawalawe National Park, known for its large herds of wild elephants that are easily spotted against the park's open landscape. Elephants are active throughout the morning.

Visit Minneriya National Park during the dry season (July-Oct) to witness "The Gathering," a large congregation of hundreds of wild elephants. Late afternoon is best as elephants emerge from the jungle to the water's edge.

Take a boat trip from Mirissa into the deep seas for a chance to see majestic blue whales and sperm whales in their natural habitat. Includes safety briefing, 3-4 hours at sea, and light refreshments.

Hike through Sinharaja Rainforest, a UNESCO World Heritage Site and a biodiversity hotspot, home to countless endemic birds, mammals, and reptiles. Includes guided trek with naturalist and packed lunch.

Stroll through the beautiful Peradeniya Royal Botanical Gardens near Kandy, home to a vast collection of orchids, giant bamboo, and the famous Javan Fig Tree. Best in morning before the heat and crowds.

Join a conservation project at Rekawa Beach at night to witness endangered sea turtles coming ashore to lay their eggs. Timing depends on tides and nesting season - requires patience and quiet observation.
Learn about sea turtle conservation at a hatchery along the southern coast. Includes viewing of tanks with different turtle species.
Learn about sea turtle conservation at a hatchery along the southern coast. Includes viewing of tanks with different turtle species.

activity.activity_name: Boat Safari in Gal Oya National Park
activity.description: Experience a unique boat safari in Gal Oya National Park, where you can see elephants swimming between the islands of the vast Senanayake Samudraya reservoir. Morning offers best chance of elephant sightings.

activity.activity_name: Bird Watching in Bundala National Park
activity.description: Spot a huge variety of migratory and resident water birds in Bundala National Park, a Ramsar wetland of international importance. Early morning is prime birding time.

Go on a safari in Wilpattu National Park, Sri Lanka's largest national park, known for its unique "villus" (natural lakes) and a more secluded wildlife experience. Requires full morning due to park size.

activity.activity_name: Visiting the Elephant Transit Home in Udawalawe
activity.description: Watch orphaned elephants being fed and cared for at the Elephant Transit Home in Udawalawe, a rehabilitation center that releases them back into the wild. Feeding times are scheduled.
Experience the thrill of white-water rafting in Kitulgala, navigating the rapids of the Kelani River surrounded by lush rainforest.
Stop at this iconic and picturesque waterfall near Ella, one of the widest falls in the country, with a plunge pool ideal for a quick dip. Can be combined with other Ella activities.
Hike through pine forests to reach Bambarakanda Falls, the tallest waterfall in Sri Lanka, which plunges 263 meters in a dramatic cascade. Includes guide and time for photography.
Wander through these cool-climate gardens in Nuwara Eliya, which once served as a trial ground for cinchona and tea cultivation. Best in morning for crisp mountain air.

activity.activity_name: Bird Watching at Kumana National Park
activity.description: Visit this premier bird sanctuary, a 200-hectare park within Yala, to see thousands of birds, including kingfishers, pelicans, and painted storks. Best during migration season (April-July).
For experienced and adventurous travelers, some operators offer the opportunity to snorkel with whales in the deep seas under strict guidance. Requires excellent swimming ability.

activity.activity_name: Scenic Train Ride - Kandy to Ella
activity.description: Take the train from Kandy to Ella. Scenic Train Ride (Badulla to Ella),Experience the final and arguably most scenic leg of the hill country railway, winding through lush greenery and mountains.
Climb the ancient rock fortress with frescoes and lion gate. UNESCO World Heritage site with breathtaking views from the summit.

activity.activity_name: Explore Temple of the Tooth Relic
activity.description: Sacred Buddhist temple housing the tooth relic of Lord Buddha. Experience traditional rituals and architecture.
Visit five magnificent cave temples with over 150 Buddha statues and intricate ceiling paintings.
Explore the medieval capital with royal palaces, temples, and the famous Gal Vihara rock statues.
Discover ancient Buddhist civilization with massive stupas, monasteries, and the sacred Bodhi Tree.
Walk through colonial-era fort with Dutch architecture, museums, boutiques, and ocean views.
Explore historic Portuguese-Dutch fort with distinctive star-shaped design and coastal views.
Ancient Hindu temple on Swami Rock cliff with spectacular ocean views and religious significance.
Important Hindu temple complex with colorful gopuram and annual festival.
Sacred mountain pilgrimage with footprint mark. Sunrise views are spectacular.
Best place to spot leopards, elephants, sloth bears, and crocodiles. Morning safari recommended.
Excellent elephant sightings year-round. Also home to water buffalo, deer, and many bird species.
Famous for "The Gathering" of hundreds of elephants from July-October.
Largest national park with natural lakes (villus). Known for leopard sightings.
Ramsar wetland with migratory flamingos and over 200 bird species.
Bird sanctuary with mangroves, lagoons, and nesting water birds. Good for crocodile spotting.
See rescued elephants being fed and bathed in the river. Best time is feeding and bath times.
Learn about sea turtle conservation and see hatchlings being released.
Drive-through safari park with animals from different continents.
UNESCO Biosphere Reserve with endemic birds, mammals, and incredible biodiversity.
Marine national park with coral reefs and colorful fish. Crystal clear waters.
Spot blue whales, sperm whales, and dolphins. Season November-April.
World-class point break surf spot. Main point suitable for intermediate to advanced surfers.
Popular surf beach with reef and point breaks. Good for beginners and intermediates.
Beginner-friendly bay beach with long gentle waves. Surf schools available.
Palm-fringed crescent beach with golden sand. Swimming, sunbathing, and beach cafes.
Beautiful horseshoe bay with coral reef. Swimming, snorkeling, and beach restaurants.
Jet skiing, banana boats, water skiing, and windsurfing on calm lagoon and beach.
Long white sand beach with calm, clear waters. Perfect for swimming.
Shallow, calm bay with knee-deep water extending far out. Ideal for families.
Perfect conditions for kitesurfing with shallow lagoon and consistent wind.
Spot large pods of spinner dolphins in their natural habitat.
Scenic hike through tea plantations and forest with panoramic views at summit.
Easy hike with stunning views of Ella Gap and surrounding mountains.

activity.activity_name: Horton Plains and World's End Trek
activity.description: Trek through cloud forest to dramatic cliff edge with 1000m drop. Start early before clouds roll in.
Multi-hour trek through misty mountains, waterfalls, and remote villages. UNESCO heritage site.
Climb massive rock for spectacular views of Sigiriya Rock and surrounding jungle.
Visit Sri Lanka's tallest waterfall (263m). Trek through pine forest to the base.
Popular multi-tiered waterfall with pool for swimming. Mythological significance.
Second highest waterfall with natural pools at different levels. Swim with views.
Trek through rainforest with streams, small waterfalls, and diverse flora.
Challenging climb to remote peak with panoramic views of Knuckles range.
Ancient forest monastery with medicinal plants and unique rock formations.
Trek through tea estates to summit with views of eastern plains.
Historic tea factory tour with tea tasting and explanation of tea processing.
Working tea estate with factory tours and scenic views of tea gardens.
Smaller tea factory with personalized tours and excellent tea tasting.
Thomas Lipton's original tea factory. Spectacular views and historic exhibits.
"Virgin White Tea" estate where tea is touched by gloved hands only. Small factory.
Join tea pluckers to learn how to pick tea leaves. Wear a basket on your back.
World's most beautiful train ride through tea plantations, mountains, and misty valleys.
Iconic train ride along the coast with ocean views from the window.
Short but scenic journey through tunnels and tea country with stunning views.
Most scenic section of the main line with nine tunnels and dramatic scenery.
Explore colonial buildings, Pettah market, Gangaramaya Temple, and Galle Face Green.
Eclectic temple with museum, architecture blending styles, and sacred relics.
Iconic red and white striped mosque in Pettah. Photography allowed outside.
Sri Lanka's largest museum with royal regalia, artifacts, and historical exhibits.
Largest park in Colombo with giant Buddha statue, fountain, and children's area.
Historic memorial hall with colonial architecture and green space for relaxation.
Scenic walk around artificial lake in heart of Kandy with mountain views.
Panoramic viewpoint overlooking Kandy Lake and Temple of the Tooth.
Vibrant market with fresh produce, spices, and unique northern Sri Lankan products.
Magnificent Hindu temple with golden gopuram and colorful deities.
Learn to prepare traditional Sri Lankan curry, hoppers, and sambols with local family.
Sample kottu roti, isso vade, and short eats at local food stalls.
Explore Dutch-era buildings while sampling local delicacies and fusion cuisine.
Taste authentic Jaffna-style crab curry at local restaurant.
Sample different grades of Ceylon tea with expert guidance.
Visit spice garden to see cinnamon, cardamom, pepper, and vanilla growing.
Traditional Sri Lankan oil massage and wellness treatment.
Beachfront yoga class suitable for all levels.
Consultation with qualified Ayurvedic doctor for personalized wellness plan.
Traditional herbal steam treatment for detoxification and relaxation.
Learn mindfulness meditation with resident monks.
Traditional games, sweetmeats, and cultural activities in April.
Buddhist festival with lanterns, pandals, and alms-giving in May.
Festival of lights celebrated by Tamil community.
Multi-religious festival with fire walking and rituals.
One of Sri Lanka's most beautiful waterfalls, 97m high. View from main road.
"Little Niagara of Sri Lanka" with twin falls dropping 80m.
Powerful waterfall (126m) with hydroelectric station nearby.
Leaf-shaped waterfall popular with locals for swimming and picnics.
Iconic waterfall (64m) with scenic approach through jungle.
17th-century Dutch mansion with colonial artifacts and history exhibits.
Learn about Sri Lanka's gem industry and see precious stones.
Historic locomotives and railway memorabilia in original station.
Folk museum in house of famous Sri Lankan author.
Ancient artifacts and carvings from the sacred city.
Massive 7 ancient Buddha statues carved into rock face, dating to 10th century.
Tallest standing Buddha statue in Sri Lanka (11m) carved from limestone.
Remote park with ancient reservoirs, elephants, and indigenous communities.

activity.activity_name: Gal Oya National Park Boat Safari
activity.description: Only park in Sri Lanka with boat safaris to see elephants swimming.
Ancient cave temple complex on massive rock with Buddha statues and paintings.
Less-visited park with elephants, water buffalo, and birdlife.
Ancient forest monastery with meditation caves and stupas on rocky peaks.
Remote park famous for wild elephants and endemic birds.
Marine national park with pristine coral reefs and abundant fish.
Ancient Buddhist temple on island off Jaffna, accessible by ferry.
Guided mountain bike tours through tea plantations and villages.
Vibrant Hindu temple with golden gopuram and colorful deities. One of the most significant Hindu temples in Sri Lanka, dating back to 948 AD.
Ancient Hindu temple, one of the oldest on the island with classical antiquity connections. One of the five ancient Ishwarams dedicated to Shiva.
Windswept island with ancient baobab trees (including 700-year-old specimen), wild donkeys roaming freely, and views of Adam's Bridge (Rama's Bridge).
Island known for wild ponies (descendants of horses left by Portuguese), coral walls, and pristine beaches. Accessible by ferry from Jaffna. Ancient Baobab tree and Dutch fort ruins.
Ancient Hindu temple with freshwater springs right by the ocean, believed to have healing properties. Natural pool where locals bathe before prayers.
Bird sanctuary with lagoon, mangrove swamps, seagrass beds, and diverse dry zone flora. Home to migratory birds and mammals including elephants and spotted deer.
Church half-buried in sand, unique architectural remnant from colonial period. Located near Jaffna Fort, offers unique photo opportunities.
Iconic library symbolizing Jaffna's renaissance after civil war. Stunning architecture and important collection of Tamil literature.
Visit working gem mines in Sri Lanka's gem capital. See traditional mining methods, pan for gems, and visit cutting facilities. Sapphires, rubies, cat's eye found here.
Try your hand at traditional gem panning in rivers near Elahera gem fields. Learn to identify raw gemstones from local experts.
Visit the only place in the world where blue moonstone is found. See traditional mining pits and learn about this unique gemstone.
Comprehensive exhibit of Sri Lankan gemstones with live cutting and polishing demonstrations. Learn to distinguish precious from semi-precious stones.
Tour historic gem trading center with Arab traders. Visit cutting workshops and learn about the gem trade from source to market.
Hands-on experience learning how raw gems are cut, shaped, and polished. Take home your own polished stone.
98-meter waterfall accessible via Dehigasthenna or Kalawaldeniya villages. Nature trails through Poonagala Forest Reserve with opportunities for camping at Upper Aberdeen 7 Falls.
Stay with local family with viewing deck overlooking Aberdeen Falls. Home garden filled with cash crops supporting endemic flora. Experience authentic village life.
Traditional kiosk with wattle and daub walls serving locally made halapa (steamed rice flour snack) wrapped in kenda kola and kithul jaggery. Authentic local flavors.
Camp at one of the estate's highest points with panoramic views of mist-covered hills and tea plantations. Explore colonial-era tea trails and access Peacock Rock views.
Traditional village with history as Prince Dutugemunu's exile haven. Terraced fields, traditional farming with buffaloes. Visit nearby Dehadhukadulla and Morape Kataragama Devalaye.
Ancient waterspout (pihilla) with astonishing water flow speed. Example of eco-friendly sustainable farming. Local kiosks selling spices, jaggery, honey.
Remote village deep in Knuckles Mountain Range. No mobile coverage, limited electricity - perfect digital detox. Experience authentic village life, traditional farming methods, and local cooking.
Towering peak steeped in legend. Challenging trek from Meemure village with spectacular views. Spot peacocks dancing in paddy fields and diverse birdlife. Requires local guide.
155 sq km conservation area with 5 major forest types. Home to slender loris, mouse deer, giant squirrel, and 17 of Sri Lanka's 23 endemic bird species. Multi-day trekking options.
Mist-covered valleys, hiking trails, scenic viewpoints. Emerging destination for immersive nature experiences with waterfalls and diverse flora.
Hidden hill-country beauty with mist-covered valleys and hiking trails that rival more well-known areas. Tea plantations and stunning viewpoints without the crowds.
One of Asia's best kitesurfing spots. Shallow lagoon with consistent wind conditions from May-September. Schools and equipment rental available.
Explore mangroves and calm lagoon waters by kayak. Spot birds, monitor lizards, and maybe even dolphins entering the lagoon.
Explore lagoon surrounded by cinnamon plantations. Visit Cinnamon Island and spice gardens. Temple and meditation center on island. Bird watching opportunities.
Museum celebrating Sri Lanka's literary and cultural heritage in writer's hometown. Traditional artifacts, folk art, and exhibits on local life.
Temple 11km northeast of Colombo with murals by artist Walimuni Solias Mendis who dedicated 20 years to paintings. Stupa magical at dusk. Important Buddhist pilgrimage site.
Two quaint forts - one built in 1560 (Dutch Fort), other star-shaped (Star Fort). Visit Dutch Reformed Church, Buddhist island temple, and southernmost tip with oldest lighthouse nearby.
Only place in Sri Lanka for boat safaris watching elephants swimming between islands in Senanayake Samudraya, the country's largest reservoir. Unique wildlife experience.
Largest national park in Sri Lanka, yet fewer visitors than Yala. Government-run bungalows available for immersive jungle experience with cook and tracker. Known for leopard sightings.
Between July and October, largest gathering of Asian elephants in the world converges here. Elephants bring calves to graze, drink, and bathe. Spectacular wildlife viewing.
Ancient monastery hidden within dense forest reserve. Stone pathways, meditation platforms, and ruins dating back 2000 years. Medicinal plants in surrounding forest. Peaceful and mystical.

Whimsical trails through tea plantations. Demodara railway station looks like a fairytale. See unique spiral railway loop where the track passes under itself. 4km hike to Nine Arch Bridge through villages.
Five-bedroomed colonial bungalow with dreamy views of surrounding tea country. Step back in time with vintage furnishings and old-world charm.
Second highest waterfall in Sri Lanka (220m). Hike to top for natural infinity pools with stunning valley views. Multiple tiers with swimming opportunities at different levels.
Panoramic viewpoint once frequented by Sir Thomas Lipton, founder of Lipton Tea. Spectacular views of surrounding hills and tea estates stretching to the southern coast on clear days.

Traditional village with no electricity/WiFi/AC. Opens seasonally for authentic yoga and Ayurveda. Organic meals cooked over open fire, treatments prescribed individually following ancient texts. Transformative experience.
Mountain lodge offering insight into disappearing way of life and natural ecosystem. Yoga with jungle views, Ayurvedic treatments using local herbs, and meditation in pristine nature.
11-meter-tall freestanding Buddha sculpture made of crystalline limestone. Dating to 7th century, once tallest free-standing Buddha in the world. Hidden in monkey-filled jungle.

Explore stunning mangrove ecosystem with 64 islands. Visit Buddhist temples, cinnamon plantations, and traditional fishing communities. Rich biodiversity with monitor lizards, birds, and aquatic life.
Boat ride through mangroves along Bentota River. Spot water monitors, crocodiles, and diverse bird species. Visit traditional villages and see cinnamon peeling demonstrations.
Traditional lagoon fishing experience with local fishermen. Learn about lagoon ecosystem and try your hand at net fishing. See stilt fishing and colorful fishing boats.
Ancient rock fortress and capital briefly in 13th century. Impressive stone stairway with carved railings. Cave temples at base with Buddhist paintings. Panoramic views from summit.
Archaeological site with remains of medieval kingdom. Circular palace complex, Buddhist shrines, and museum with artifacts. Dating to 12th century.
Important archaeological site with ruins from medieval period. Birthplace of King Parakramabahu I. Ancient stupa and inscriptions.
Scenic Buddhist temple on rocky outcrop overlooking the ocean. Legend of Princess Vihara Maha Devi. Stunning coastal views and peaceful atmosphere.
